segments.text: This 30-year-old trend is reversing and IITs are reversing the brain drain. For decades, IIT graduates saw the US as the
default destination. Get into Stanford or MIT, land a job at Google or Microsoft, get an H1B visa, build a
career in Silicon Valley. Now, this is changing. At IIT Bombay's 2023 to 24
placement season, only 78 out of 1475 accepted job offers came from international companies. One catalyst
for this change is Trump's unpredictable policies over H1B visa rules. This isn't just pushing people away from the US, it's pulling them towards India.
LinkedIn data shows a 40% increase in tech professionals changing their location to India in Q3 2025. Global
companies that used to hire Indian engineers on H1Bs are now setting up tech centers in India instead. One
company ANSR established 38 in-house tech centers in India in just the last 12 months. On the face of it, India is more prepared to absorb this talent.
Infrastructure is stronger, capital is more abundant, startups are bolder, but bureaucratic inertia is still real. It's slow and complicated for startups. What
do you think? Can India attract this talent and capitalize on a rare moment? Let me know in the comments.
